fragility_points: A fast risk-off move into U.S. morning on April 17.
Binance-specific print anomaly or liquidity disruption near noon ET.
key_assumptions: No fresh bearish macro or crypto catalyst drives a >6% selloff before noon ET Friday.
Binance market functioning remains normal into the resolving minute.
main_logical_chain: Contract resolves on one exact Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 17.
Current Binance spot is materially above 2200 and recent sampled range stayed above the threshold.
No clearly identified scheduled catalyst looks large enough on its own to erase that cushion before resolution.
Residual risk remains because a sharp selloff into the exact resolving minute could still flip the market to No.
main_thesis: ETH is likely to stay above 2200 at the Binance noon ET resolving minute, but the single-minute close mechanic keeps residual downside timing risk nontrivial.
quote_anchors: Polymarket: resolves to Yes if Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 ET has final Close higher than 2200.
source_quality_view: Source-of-truth ambiguity is low because the contract is explicit, but evidence independence is only medium because the thesis leans heavily on Binance and contract mechanics.
strongest_disconfirmers: Single-minute resolution creates path-sensitive downside risk even if ETH trades above 2200 most of the time.
Crypto can move more than 6% intraday on shock headlines or liquidation.
strongest_supports: Binance ETH/USDT last price sampled at 2353.84, well above 2200.
Binance 24h low sampled at 2308.50, still above 2200.
Polymarket contract wording clearly points to Binance ETH/USDT 12:00 ET 1m close as the governing source.
timing_relevance: This case is mainly about whether the current >2200 cushion survives into the exact noon ET resolving minute, not about ETH direction in a broad daily sense.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether any unscheduled macro or crypto headline emerges before the resolving window.
what_would_change_view: A decisive break toward/below 2300, a new bearish catalyst, or Binance-specific instability near resolution would cut the Yes probability materially.
